key_points: Heron’s formula is used to find the area of a triangle when the lengths of all three sides are known.
Let a, b, c be the sides of a triangle. Semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2.
For a right-angled triangle, Heron’s formula gives the same area as ½ × base × height.
For an equilateral triangle of side a, Area = (√3/4) × a².
exam_tips: Check if triangle is right-angled; then simpler formula ½×base×height can be used.
For decimal sides, carefully handle multiplication under square root.
Use Heron’s formula to also find inradius: r = Area/s.
Double-check units and square root calculation to avoid mistakes.
